title: tetragonolobus erectus
intro: Tetragonolobus erectus, also known as horse gram, is a legume plant native to Asia and Australia, characterized by its erect growth habit and small, yellowish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of protein, fiber, and polyphenols. Consuming horse gram is said to provide several health benefits, such as reducing blood sugar levels, improving digestion, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, horse gram is often used to make teas, soups, and powder supplements, which can be consumed for its medicinal and nutritional properties.
